column2: parātum cor meum; cantābō et psallēbō in glōriā meā. ^ My heart is ready
column4: my heart is ready; I will sing and give praise with my glory.

column3: psaltērium et cittara; exsurgēbō dīlūculō. ^ Arise
column5: psaltery and harp; I will arise in the morning early.

column4: ^ I will praise thee among the people
column6: and I will sing unto thee among the nations

column1: 5 ^ quia magna est super caelōs misericordia tua
column2: et usque ad nūbēs vēritās tua. ^ for thy mercy is great above the heavens
column3: and thy truth even unto the clouds.
et super omnem terram glōria tua
column4: ^ Be thou exalted above the heavens
column6: and thy glory over all the earth

column1: 7 ^ ut līberentur dīlectī tuī; salvum fac dexterā tuā
column2: et exaudī mē. ^ that thy beloved ones may be delivered; save with thy right hand and hear me.

column1: 8 ^ Deus locūtus est in sānctō suō: Exsultābō
column3: et convallem tabernāculōrum dīmētībor. ^ God hath spoken in his holiness: I will rejoice
column4: and I will divide Sichem and I will mete out the vale of tabernacles.

column3: et Efraim susceptiō capitis meī; Jūda rēx meus. ^ Gilead is mine
column5: and Ephraim the protection of my head; Judah is my king.

column1: 10 ^ Moab lebēs speī meae; in Idūmaeam extendēbō calceāmentum meum; mihi aliēnigenae amīcī factī sunt. ^ Moab is the pot of my hope; over Edom I will stretch out my shoe; the aliens are become my friends.
11 ^ Quis dēdūcēbit mē in cīvitātem mūnītam? Quis dēdūcēbit mē usque in Idūmaeam? ^ Who will bring me into the strong city? Who will lead me into Edom?

column3: quī repulistī nōs? Et nōn exībis
column5: in virtūtibus nostrīs? ^ Wilt not thou
column7: who hast cast us off? And wilt not thou

column2: quia vāna salūs hominis. ^ O grant us help from trouble
column3: for vain is the help of man.

column2: et ipse ad nihilum dēdūcēbit inimīcōs nostrōs. ^ Through God we shall do mightily
column3: and he will bring our enemies to nothing.
